heading: Today
text: Early clouds giving way to sunshine.  Increasing afternoon clouds, with a few spotty rain showers north.  Breezy and mild.  Highs in the low to mid 50s north, upper 50s to low 60s south.  Winds south to southwest 10 to 15 mph, gusting to 25 mph, shifting to the west.

heading: Tonight
text: Becoming cloudy. A rising chance of rain showers after midnight, with some pockets of freezing rain in the St. Lawrence Valley.  Lows in the 30s to low 40s from the Adirondacks and Rt. 4 north, in the 40s south.  Winds west, becoming north, north of Rt. 4, diminishing to light and variable.

heading: Tuesday
text: Pockets of freezing rain or sleet near and north of the Quebec border, then periods of rain.  Highs from the 30s to low 40s from the Adirondacks and Rt. 4 north, upper 40s and 50s south.  Light winds, mainly east from the Adirondacks and Rt. 4 north, to south for areas south of Rt. 4, near 10 mph.
